In 1663 (Kanbun 3), the shrine was restored by Grand Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaga Village, Okatani. {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} later criticized this restoration site as inappropriate in his work ''Ninomiya Kansha Engaku Kō'', proposing that the original site was within Yamaga Village at the remains of the former {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which had been merged in 1908 (Meiji 41)Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.201–202. The current location was established in 1876 (Meiji 9). The shrine building was reconstructed in 1922 (Taishō 11). == Rituals == On the 16th day of the 3rd lunar month, a ritual known as ''Miyatamori Shinji'' (宮田もり神事) is held, which is associated with {{ill|Kōshin belief|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1991): p.293. During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (November), rituals are conducted in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (January), {{ill|Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (February 11), {{ill|Fūhi no Sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (May and August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (December 23), remote rituals are performed at the Gojōden of the Ise Grand ShrineShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.200–201. == Access == Near the shrine are the ruins of an ancient icehouse (氷室) associated with the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. About 2.5 km from Kamo Shrine is the {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which enshrines the same deity and is located within the same Shirota region. ; By Public Transport There are no nearby train stations or bus stops. From {{ill|Tamaru Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is about a 50-minute walk (4.0 km) southwest, or about 53 minutes (4.3 km) south from {{ill|Sotojōda Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. From Yamaga village, a stone marker labeled "Kamo Shrine" leads through a bamboo grove, under a tunnel beneath the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and along a mountain path to the shrine. The distance from the marker to the shrine is about 1.1 kmIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.63, taking around 20 minutes. There are forestry roads nearby, so caution is advised to avoid getting lost. ; By Car From {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Ise Expressway, it takes about 6 minutes (2.1 km) to reach the stone marker for Kamo Shrine. The mountain path beyond the marker is for pedestrians only, and vehicles cannot pass through. However, there is a parking area near {{ill|Matoyama Park|ja|的山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is accessible via a tunnel under the Ise Expressway. This route includes a paved mountain path, reducing the walking distance. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_05.jpg 鴨神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0704.html 鴨神社（かもじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨神社|version=103489105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:鴨神社]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:鴨神社 (玉城町)]]

{{神社 |名称= 鴨神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kamo-jinja (Tamaki) 01.jpg|270px|鴨神社]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]山神[[小字|字]]岡谷1398 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 40.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 27.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 石己呂和居命
御前神 |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 鴨の谷様 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a {{ill|sessha|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}) (Inner Shrine). It is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|Mount Dainichi (Ise)|ja|大日山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Dainichi (Ise)|qq=}}, and is considered the most challenging {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} to reach among the {{ill|125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/125 Shrines of Ise|qq=}}{{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}} (2013): p.69. == Overview == {{ill|Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}} is located at 1398 Okatani, Yamaga, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.201. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) located in Tamaki Town and Ranks 4th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.9–11. The "Kamo" in the shrine name was traditionally believed to refer to an old place name. However, archaeological progress at the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}} site has led to suggestions of a connection with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, who served as water administratorsShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.199. The shrine grounds cover an area of 15,018 m²Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.202. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with a wooden plank roof, facing south, and is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}}. It features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=}}. The sacred object (shintai) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. There is no offertory box. A stream flowing beneath the shrine grounds is said to have never dried up, even during droughtsIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.65. The trees on the grounds display beautiful autumn foliage during late autumn. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are '''Ishikorowake no Mikoto''' (石己呂和居命) and '''Mimae no Kami''' (御前神). Both deities are children of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=}} and are considered gods of agriculture and irrigation. Originally, there were separate shrine buildings for each deityUjiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10, but after the shrine was restored by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} during the Edo period, the deities have been enshrined together in a single structure. == History == According to the ''Enryaku Gishiki-chō'', the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p.68, giving it a history as long as the Ise Grand Shrine itselfShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.200. In ancient texts, the shrine is referred to as "Kamo-sha" or "Kamo Jinja," and its reading as "Kamo" is undisputed. It was designated to undergo reconstruction every 20 years, following the tradition of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} at the Ise Grand Shrine. However, records of reconstruction ceased after an entry from December 8, 1340, which mentions an order to rebuild the shrine using rice tributes from Tajima Province. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), the shrine was restored by Grand Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} in Yamaga Village, Okatani. {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} later criticized this restoration site as inappropriate in his work ''Ninomiya Kansha Engaku Kō'', proposing that the original site was within Yamaga Village at the remains of the former {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}, which had been merged in 1908 (Meiji 41)Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.201–202. The current location was established in 1876 (Meiji 9). The shrine building was reconstructed in 1922 (Taishō 11). == Rituals == On the 16th day of the 3rd lunar month, a ritual known as ''Miyatamori Shinji'' (宮田もり神事) is held, which is associated with {{ill|Kōshin belief|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin belief|qq=}}Sakurai (1991): p.293. During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} (November), rituals are conducted in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January), {{ill|Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Fūhi no Sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fūhi no Sai|qq=}} (May and August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23), remote rituals are performed at the Gojōden of the Ise Grand ShrineShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.200–201. == Access == Near the shrine are the ruins of an ancient icehouse (氷室) associated with the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. About 2.5 km from Kamo Shrine is the {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deity and is located within the same Shirota region. ; By Public Transport There are no nearby train stations or bus stops. From {{ill|Tamaru Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Sangū Line|qq=}}, it is about a 50-minute walk (4.0 km) southwest, or about 53 minutes (4.3 km) south from {{ill|Sotojōda Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotojōda Station|qq=}}. From Yamaga village, a stone marker labeled "Kamo Shrine" leads through a bamboo grove, under a tunnel beneath the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, and along a mountain path to the shrine. The distance from the marker to the shrine is about 1.1 kmIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.63, taking around 20 minutes. There are forestry roads nearby, so caution is advised to avoid getting lost. ; By Car From {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the Ise Expressway, it takes about 6 minutes (2.1 km) to reach the stone marker for Kamo Shrine. The mountain path beyond the marker is for pedestrians only, and vehicles cannot pass through. However, there is a parking area near {{ill|Matoyama Park|ja|的山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoyama Park|qq=}}, which is accessible via a tunnel under the Ise Expressway. This route includes a paved mountain path, reducing the walking distance. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_05.jpg 鴨神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0704.html 鴨神社（かもじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨神社|version=103489105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:鴨神社]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:鴨神社 (玉城町)]]

However, archaeological progress at the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}} site has led to suggestions of a connection with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, who served as water administratorsShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.199. The shrine grounds cover an area of 15,018 m²Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.202. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with a wooden plank roof, facing south, and is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}}. It features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=}}. The sacred object (shintai) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. There is no offertory box. A stream flowing beneath the shrine grounds is said to have never dried up, even during droughtsIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.65. The trees on the grounds display beautiful autumn foliage during late autumn. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are '''Ishikorowake no Mikoto''' (石己呂和居命) and '''Mimae no Kami''' (御前神). Both deities are children of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=}} and are considered gods of agriculture and irrigation. Originally, there were separate shrine buildings for each deityUjiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10, but after the shrine was restored by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} during the Edo period, the deities have been enshrined together in a single structure. == History == According to the ''Enryaku Gishiki-chō'', the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p.68, giving it a history as long as the Ise Grand Shrine itselfShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.200. In ancient texts, the shrine is referred to as "Kamo-sha" or "Kamo Jinja," and its reading as "Kamo" is undisputed. It was designated to undergo reconstruction every 20 years, following the tradition of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} at the Ise Grand Shrine. However, records of reconstruction ceased after an entry from December 8, 1340, which mentions an order to rebuild the shrine using rice tributes from Tajima Province. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), the shrine was restored by Grand Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} in Yamaga Village, Okatani. {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} later criticized this restoration site as inappropriate in his work ''Ninomiya Kansha Engaku Kō'', proposing that the original site was within Yamaga Village at the remains of the former {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}, which had been merged in 1908 (Meiji 41)Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.201–202. The current location was established in 1876 (Meiji 9). The shrine building was reconstructed in 1922 (Taishō 11). == Rituals == On the 16th day of the 3rd lunar month, a ritual known as ''Miyatamori Shinji'' (宮田もり神事) is held, which is associated with {{ill|Kōshin belief|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin belief|qq=}}Sakurai (1991): p.293. During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} (November), rituals are conducted in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January), {{ill|Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Fūhi no Sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fūhi no Sai|qq=}} (May and August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23), remote rituals are performed at the Gojōden of the Ise Grand ShrineShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.200–201. == Access == Near the shrine are the ruins of an ancient icehouse (氷室) associated with the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. About 2.5 km from Kamo Shrine is the {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deity and is located within the same Shirota region. ; By Public Transport There are no nearby train stations or bus stops. From {{ill|Tamaru Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Sangū Line|qq=}}, it is about a 50-minute walk (4.0 km) southwest, or about 53 minutes (4.3 km) south from {{ill|Sotojōda Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotojōda Station|qq=}}. From Yamaga village, a stone marker labeled "Kamo Shrine" leads through a bamboo grove, under a tunnel beneath the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, and along a mountain path to the shrine. The distance from the marker to the shrine is about 1.1 kmIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.63, taking around 20 minutes. There are forestry roads nearby, so caution is advised to avoid getting lost. ; By Car From {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the Ise Expressway, it takes about 6 minutes (2.1 km) to reach the stone marker for Kamo Shrine. The mountain path beyond the marker is for pedestrians only, and vehicles cannot pass through. However, there is a parking area near {{ill|Matoyama Park|ja|的山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoyama Park|qq=}}, which is accessible via a tunnel under the Ise Expressway. This route includes a paved mountain path, reducing the walking distance. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 鴨の谷様 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a {{ill|sessha|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}) (Inner Shrine). It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) located in Tamaki Town and Ranks 4th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.9–11. It features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=}}. The sacred object (shintai) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. There is no offertory box. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_05.jpg 鴨神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0704.html 鴨神社（かもじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨神社|version=103489105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:鴨神社]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:鴨神社 (玉城町)]]

{{神社 |名称= 鴨神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kamo-jinja (Tamaki) 01.jpg|270px|鴨神社]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]山神[[小字|字]]岡谷1398 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 40.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 27.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 石己呂和居命
御前神 |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 鴨の谷様 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a {{ill|sessha|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}) (Inner Shrine). It is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|Mount Dainichi (Ise)|ja|大日山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Dainichi (Ise)|qq=}}, and is considered the most challenging {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} to reach among the {{ill|125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/125 Shrines of Ise|qq=}}{{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}} (2013): p.69. == Overview == {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}} is located at 1398 Okatani, Yamaga, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.201. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) located in Tamaki Town and Ranks 4th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.9–11. The "Kamo" in the shrine name was traditionally believed to refer to an old place name. However, archaeological progress at the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}} site has led to suggestions of a connection with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, who served as water administratorsShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.199. The shrine grounds cover an area of 15,018 m²Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.202. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with a wooden plank roof, facing south, and is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}}. It features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=}}. The sacred object (shintai) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. There is no offertory box. A stream flowing beneath the shrine grounds is said to have never dried up, even during droughtsIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.65. The trees on the grounds display beautiful autumn foliage during late autumn. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are '''Ishikorowake no Mikoto''' (石己呂和居命) and '''Mimae no Kami''' (御前神). Both deities are children of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=}} and are considered gods of agriculture and irrigation. Originally, there were separate shrine buildings for each deityUjiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10, but after the shrine was restored by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} during the Edo period, the deities have been enshrined together in a single structure. == History == According to the ''Enryaku Gishiki-chō'', the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p.68, giving it a history as long as the Ise Grand Shrine itselfShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.200. In ancient texts, the shrine is referred to as "Kamo-sha" or "Kamo Jinja," and its reading as "Kamo" is undisputed. It was designated to undergo reconstruction every 20 years, following the tradition of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} at the Ise Grand Shrine. However, records of reconstruction ceased after an entry from December 8, 1340, which mentions an order to rebuild the shrine using rice tributes from Tajima Province. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), the shrine was restored by Grand Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} in Yamaga Village, Okatani. {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} later criticized this restoration site as inappropriate in his work ''Ninomiya Kansha Engaku Kō'', proposing that the original site was within Yamaga Village at the remains of the former {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}, which had been merged in 1908 (Meiji 41)Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.201–202. The current location was established in 1876 (Meiji 9). The shrine building was reconstructed in 1922 (Taishō 11). == Rituals == On the 16th day of the 3rd lunar month, a ritual known as ''Miyatamori Shinji'' (宮田もり神事) is held, which is associated with {{ill|Kōshin belief|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin belief|qq=}}Sakurai (1991): p.293. During the {{ill|Kinensai|lt=Kinensai|lt=Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} (November), rituals are conducted in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January), {{ill|Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Fūhi no Sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fūhi no Sai|qq=}} (May and August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23), remote rituals are performed at the Gojōden of the Ise Grand ShrineShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.200–201. == Access == Near the shrine are the ruins of an ancient icehouse (氷室) associated with the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. About 2.5 km from Kamo Shrine is the {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deity and is located within the same Shirota region. ; By Public Transport There are no nearby train stations or bus stops. From {{ill|Tamaru Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Sangū Line|qq=}}, it is about a 50-minute walk (4.0 km) southwest, or about 53 minutes (4.3 km) south from {{ill|Sotojōda Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotojōda Station|qq=}}. From Yamaga village, a stone marker labeled "Kamo Shrine" leads through a bamboo grove, under a tunnel beneath the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, and along a mountain path to the shrine. The distance from the marker to the shrine is about 1.1 kmIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.63, taking around 20 minutes. There are forestry roads nearby, so caution is advised to avoid getting lost. ; By Car From {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the Ise Expressway, it takes about 6 minutes (2.1 km) to reach the stone marker for Kamo Shrine. The mountain path beyond the marker is for pedestrians only, and vehicles cannot pass through. However, there is a parking area near {{ill|Matoyama Park|ja|的山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoyama Park|qq=}}, which is accessible via a tunnel under the Ise Expressway. This route includes a paved mountain path, reducing the walking distance. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_05.jpg 鴨神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0704.html 鴨神社（かもじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨神社|version=103489105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:鴨神社]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:鴨神社 (玉城町)]]

The current location was established in 1876 (Meiji 9). The shrine building was reconstructed in 1922 (Taishō 11). == Rituals == On the 16th day of the 3rd lunar month, a ritual known as ''Miyatamori Shinji'' (宮田もり神事) is held, which is associated with {{ill|Kōshin belief|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin belief|qq=}}Sakurai (1991): p.293. During the {{ill|Kinensai|lt=Kinensai|lt=Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} (November), rituals are conducted in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January), {{ill|Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Fūhi no Sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fūhi no Sai|qq=}} (May and August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23), remote rituals are performed at the Gojōden of the Ise Grand ShrineShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.200–201. == Access == Near the shrine are the ruins of an ancient icehouse (氷室) associated with the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. About 2.5 km from Kamo Shrine is the {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deity and is located within the same Shirota region. ; By Public Transport There are no nearby train stations or bus stops. From {{ill|Tamaru Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Sangū Line|qq=}}, it is about a 50-minute walk (4.0 km) southwest, or about 53 minutes (4.3 km) south from {{ill|Sotojōda Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotojōda Station|qq=}}. From Yamaga village, a stone marker labeled "Kamo Shrine" leads through a bamboo grove, under a tunnel beneath the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, and along a mountain path to the shrine. The distance from the marker to the shrine is about 1.1 kmIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.63, taking around 20 minutes. There are forestry roads nearby, so caution is advised to avoid getting lost. ; By Car From {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the Ise Expressway, it takes about 6 minutes (2.1 km) to reach the stone marker for Kamo Shrine. The mountain path beyond the marker is for pedestrians only, and vehicles cannot pass through. However, there is a parking area near {{ill|Matoyama Park|ja|的山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoyama Park|qq=}}, which is accessible via a tunnel under the Ise Expressway. This route includes a paved mountain path, reducing the walking distance. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_05.jpg 鴨神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0704.html 鴨神社（かもじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨神社|version=103489105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:鴨神社]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:鴨神社 (玉城町)]]

{{神社 |名称= 鴨神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kamo-jinja (Tamaki) 01.jpg|270px|鴨神社]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]山神[[小字|字]]岡谷1398 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 40.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 27.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 石己呂和居命
御前神 |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 鴨の谷様 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a {{ill|sessha|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}) (Inner Shrine). It is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|Mount Dainichi (Ise)|ja|大日山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Dainichi (Ise)|qq=}}, and is considered the most challenging {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} to reach among the {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt=125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/125 Shrines of Ise|qq=}}{{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}} (2013): p.69. == Overview == {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}} is located at 1398 Okatani, Yamaga, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.201. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) located in Tamaki Town and Ranks 4th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.9–11. The "Kamo" in the shrine name was traditionally believed to refer to an old place name. However, archaeological progress at the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}} site has led to suggestions of a connection with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, who served as water administratorsShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.199. The shrine grounds cover an area of 15,018 m²Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.202. The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with a wooden plank roof, facing south, and is enclosed by a single layer of {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagaki|qq=}}. It features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=}}. The sacred object (shintai) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. There is no offertory box. A stream flowing beneath the shrine grounds is said to have never dried up, even during droughtsIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.65. The trees on the grounds display beautiful autumn foliage during late autumn. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are '''Ishikorowake no Mikoto''' (石己呂和居命) and '''Mimae no Kami''' (御前神). Both deities are children of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=}} and are considered gods of agriculture and irrigation. Originally, there were separate shrine buildings for each deityUjiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10, but after the shrine was restored by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} during the Edo period, the deities have been enshrined together in a single structure. == History == According to the ''Enryaku Gishiki-chō'', the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}Kaneko (1983): p.68, giving it a history as long as the Ise Grand Shrine itselfShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.200. In ancient texts, the shrine is referred to as "Kamo-sha" or "Kamo Jinja," and its reading as "Kamo" is undisputed. It was designated to undergo reconstruction every 20 years, following the tradition of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} at the Ise Grand Shrine. However, records of reconstruction ceased after an entry from December 8, 1340, which mentions an order to rebuild the shrine using rice tributes from Tajima Province. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), the shrine was restored by Grand Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} in Yamaga Village, Okatani. {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} later criticized this restoration site as inappropriate in his work ''Ninomiya Kansha Engaku Kō'', proposing that the original site was within Yamaga Village at the remains of the former {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}, which had been merged in 1908 (Meiji 41)Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.201–202. The current location was established in 1876 (Meiji 9). The shrine building was reconstructed in 1922 (Taishō 11). == Rituals == On the 16th day of the 3rd lunar month, a ritual known as ''Miyatamori Shinji'' (宮田もり神事) is held, which is associated with {{ill|Kōshin belief|ja|庚申信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin belief|qq=}}Sakurai (1991): p.293. During the {{ill|Kinensai|lt=Kinensai|lt=Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinen-sai|qq=}} (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} (November), rituals are conducted in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January), {{ill|Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Fūhi no Sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fūhi no Sai|qq=}} (May and August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23), remote rituals are performed at the Gojōden of the Ise Grand ShrineShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.200–201. == Access == Near the shrine are the ruins of an ancient icehouse (氷室) associated with the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. About 2.5 km from Kamo Shrine is the {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deity and is located within the same Shirota region. ; By Public Transport There are no nearby train stations or bus stops. From {{ill|Tamaru Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaru Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Sangū Line|qq=}}, it is about a 50-minute walk (4.0 km) southwest, or about 53 minutes (4.3 km) south from {{ill|Sotojōda Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotojōda Station|qq=}}. From Yamaga village, a stone marker labeled "Kamo Shrine" leads through a bamboo grove, under a tunnel beneath the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, and along a mountain path to the shrine. The distance from the marker to the shrine is about 1.1 kmIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.63, taking around 20 minutes. There are forestry roads nearby, so caution is advised to avoid getting lost. ; By Car From {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the Ise Expressway, it takes about 6 minutes (2.1 km) to reach the stone marker for Kamo Shrine. The mountain path beyond the marker is for pedestrians only, and vehicles cannot pass through. However, there is a parking area near {{ill|Matoyama Park|ja|的山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoyama Park|qq=}}, which is accessible via a tunnel under the Ise Expressway. This route includes a paved mountain path, reducing the walking distance. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. {{神社 |名称= 鴨神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kamo-jinja (Tamaki) 01.jpg|270px|鴨神社]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]山神[[小字|字]]岡谷1398 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 40.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 27.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 石己呂和居命
御前神 |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 鴨の谷様 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} ([[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]) (Inner Shrine). It is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|Mount Dainichi (Ise)|ja|大日山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Dainichi (Ise)|qq=}}, and is considered the most challenging [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] to reach among the {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt=125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/125 Shrines of Ise|qq=}}{{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}} (2013): p.69. == Overview == {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}} is located at 1398 Okatani, Yamaga, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.201. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) located in Tamaki Town and Ranks 4th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.9–11. The "Kamo" in the shrine name was traditionally believed to refer to an old place name. However, archaeological progress at the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]] site has led to suggestions of a connection with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, who served as water administratorsShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.199. The shrine grounds cover an area of 15,018 m²Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.202. The shrine building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a wooden plank roof, facing south, and is enclosed by a single layer of [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]]. It features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=}}. The sacred object (shintai) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. There is no offertory box. A stream flowing beneath the shrine grounds is said to have never dried up, even during droughtsIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.65. The trees on the grounds display beautiful autumn foliage during late autumn. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are '''Ishikorowake no Mikoto''' (石己呂和居命) and '''Mimae no Kami''' (御前神). Both deities are children of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=}} and are considered gods of agriculture and irrigation. Originally, there were separate shrine buildings for each deityUjiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10, but after the shrine was restored by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} during the Edo period, the deities have been enshrined together in a single structure. == History == According to the ''Enryaku Gishiki-chō'', the shrine was established during the reign of [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Kaneko (1983): p.68, giving it a history as long as the Ise Grand Shrine itselfShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.200. In ancient texts, the shrine is referred to as "Kamo-sha" or "Kamo Jinja," and its reading as "Kamo" is undisputed. It was designated to undergo reconstruction every 20 years, following the tradition of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} at the Ise Grand Shrine. However, records of reconstruction ceased after an entry from December 8, 1340, which mentions an order to rebuild the shrine using rice tributes from Tajima Province. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), the shrine was restored by Grand Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} in Yamaga Village, Okatani. {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} later criticized this restoration site as inappropriate in his work ''Ninomiya Kansha Engaku Kō'', proposing that the original site was within Yamaga Village at the remains of the former {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}, which had been merged in 1908 (Meiji 41)Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.201–202. The current location was established in 1876 (Meiji 9). The shrine building was reconstructed in 1922 (Taishō 11). == Rituals == On the 16th day of the 3rd lunar month, a ritual known as ''Miyatamori Shinji'' (宮田もり神事) is held, which is associated with [[:en:Kōshin|Kōshin belief]]Sakurai (1991): p.293. During the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November), rituals are conducted in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January), {{ill|Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Fūhi no Sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fūhi no Sai|qq=}} (May and August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23), remote rituals are performed at the Gojōden of the Ise Grand ShrineShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.200–201. == Access == Near the shrine are the ruins of an ancient icehouse (氷室) associated with the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]]. About 2.5 km from Kamo Shrine is the {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deity and is located within the same Shirota region. ; By Public Transport There are no nearby train stations or bus stops. From [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamaru Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|JR Sangū Line]], it is about a 50-minute walk (4.0 km) southwest, or about 53 minutes (4.3 km) south from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojōda Station]]. From Yamaga village, a stone marker labeled "Kamo Shrine" leads through a bamboo grove, under a tunnel beneath the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], and along a mountain path to the shrine. The distance from the marker to the shrine is about 1.1 kmIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.63, taking around 20 minutes. There are forestry roads nearby, so caution is advised to avoid getting lost. ; By Car From {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the Ise Expressway, it takes about 6 minutes (2.1 km) to reach the stone marker for Kamo Shrine. The mountain path beyond the marker is for pedestrians only, and vehicles cannot pass through. However, there is a parking area near {{ill|Matoyama Park|ja|的山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoyama Park|qq=}}, which is accessible via a tunnel under the Ise Expressway. This route includes a paved mountain path, reducing the walking distance. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_05.jpg 鴨神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0704.html 鴨神社（かもじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨神社|version=103489105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:鴨神社]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:鴨神社 (玉城町)]]

This route includes a paved mountain path, reducing the walking distance. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_05.jpg 鴨神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0704.html 鴨神社（かもじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨神社|version=103489105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:鴨神社]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:鴨神社 (玉城町)]] [[Category:加茂神社]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:同名の神社
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 鴨神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kamo-jinja (Tamaki) 01.jpg|270px|鴨神社]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]山神[[小字|字]]岡谷1398 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 40.4 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 36 | 経度秒 = 27.2 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 石己呂和居命
御前神 |社格= {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}摂社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 鴨の谷様 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kamo Shrine''' (鴨神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} ([[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]) (Inner Shrine). It is located on the mid-slope of {{ill|Mount Dainichi (Ise)|ja|大日山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Dainichi (Ise)|qq=}}, and is considered the most challenging [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] to reach among the {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|lt=125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/125 Shrines of Ise|qq=}}{{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}} (2013): p.69. == Overview == {{ill|Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo shrines|lt=Kamo Shrine|ja|鴨神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine|qq=}} is located at 1398 Okatani, Yamaga, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.201. It is one of the 13 auxiliary and subordinate shrines of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) located in Tamaki Town and Ranks 4th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.9–11. The "Kamo" in the shrine name was traditionally believed to refer to an old place name. However, archaeological progress at the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]] site has led to suggestions of a connection with the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, who served as water administratorsShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.199. The shrine grounds cover an area of 15,018 m²Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.202. The shrine building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a wooden plank roof, facing south, and is enclosed by a single layer of [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]]. It features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=}}. The sacred object (shintai) is a stoneKaneko (1983): p.59. There is no offertory box. A stream flowing beneath the shrine grounds is said to have never dried up, even during droughtsIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.65. The trees on the grounds display beautiful autumn foliage during late autumn. == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities are '''Ishikorowake no Mikoto''' (石己呂和居命) and '''Mimae no Kami''' (御前神). Both deities are children of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=}} and are considered gods of agriculture and irrigation. Originally, there were separate shrine buildings for each deityUjiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10, but after the shrine was restored by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} during the Edo period, the deities have been enshrined together in a single structure. == History == According to the ''Enryaku Gishiki-chō'', the shrine was established during the reign of [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]Kaneko (1983): p.68, giving it a history as long as the Ise Grand Shrine itselfShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.200. In ancient texts, the shrine is referred to as "Kamo-sha" or "Kamo Jinja," and its reading as "Kamo" is undisputed. It was designated to undergo reconstruction every 20 years, following the tradition of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} at the Ise Grand Shrine. However, records of reconstruction ceased after an entry from December 8, 1340, which mentions an order to rebuild the shrine using rice tributes from Tajima Province. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), the shrine was restored by Grand Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} in Yamaga Village, Okatani. {{ill|Mikado Kiyonao|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikado Kiyonao|qq=}} later criticized this restoration site as inappropriate in his work ''Ninomiya Kansha Engaku Kō'', proposing that the original site was within Yamaga Village at the remains of the former {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}}, which had been merged in 1908 (Meiji 41)Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.201–202. The current location was established in 1876 (Meiji 9). The shrine building was reconstructed in 1922 (Taishō 11). == Rituals == On the 16th day of the 3rd lunar month, a ritual known as ''Miyatamori Shinji'' (宮田もり神事) is held, which is associated with [[:en:Kōshin|Kōshin belief]]Sakurai (1991): p.293. During the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (November), rituals are conducted in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January), {{ill|Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|ja|建国記念祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkoku Kinen no Hi|qq=}} (February 11), {{ill|Fūhi no Sai|ja|風日祈祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fūhi no Sai|qq=}} (May and August), and {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|qq=}} (December 23), remote rituals are performed at the Gojōden of the Ise Grand ShrineShikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): pp.200–201. == Access == Near the shrine are the ruins of an ancient icehouse (氷室) associated with the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]]. About 2.5 km from Kamo Shrine is the {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deity and is located within the same Shirota region. ; By Public Transport There are no nearby train stations or bus stops. From [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamaru Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|JR Sangū Line]], it is about a 50-minute walk (4.0 km) southwest, or about 53 minutes (4.3 km) south from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotojōda Station]]. From Yamaga village, a stone marker labeled "Kamo Shrine" leads through a bamboo grove, under a tunnel beneath the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], and along a mountain path to the shrine. The distance from the marker to the shrine is about 1.1 kmIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.63, taking around 20 minutes. There are forestry roads nearby, so caution is advised to avoid getting lost. ; By Car From {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the Ise Expressway, it takes about 6 minutes (2.1 km) to reach the stone marker for Kamo Shrine. The mountain path beyond the marker is for pedestrians only, and vehicles cannot pass through. However, there is a parking area near {{ill|Matoyama Park|ja|的山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoyama Park|qq=}}, which is accessible via a tunnel under the Ise Expressway. This route includes a paved mountain path, reducing the walking distance. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_05.jpg 鴨神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0704.html 鴨神社（かもじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨神社|version=103489105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:鴨神社]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:鴨神社 (玉城町)]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:同名の神社]]

From Yamaga village, a stone marker labeled "Kamo Shrine" leads through a bamboo grove, under a tunnel beneath the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], and along a mountain path to the shrine. The distance from the marker to the shrine is about 1.1 kmIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.63, taking around 20 minutes. There are forestry roads nearby, so caution is advised to avoid getting lost. ; By Car From {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the Ise Expressway, it takes about 6 minutes (2.1 km) to reach the stone marker for Kamo Shrine. The mountain path beyond the marker is for pedestrians only, and vehicles cannot pass through. However, there is a parking area near {{ill|Matoyama Park|ja|的山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matoyama Park|qq=}}, which is accessible via a tunnel under the Ise Expressway. This route includes a paved mountain path, reducing the walking distance. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area04/area04_05.jpg 鴨神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0704.html 鴨神社（かもじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨神社|version=103489105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[ja:鴨神社]] [[sn:Kamo Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:鴨神社 (玉城町)]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:同名の神社]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]]
